generated_comment: Arre, har ghar mein jo jis kaam mein better ho wahi sambhalta hai. Mere husband hamesha bank, bills aur bahar ke kaam dekhte hain, aur main ghar, kitchen, bachche - isme kya galat hai, practical hi toh hai. Aajkal ke ladke thoda help kar dein toh accha lagta hai, but poora ulta system karne ki bhi kya zarurat hai?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely 'practical' rather than unequal. It encodes the surface manifestations of 'he handles finances, she handles home,' the 'he helps her' framing, and resistance to changing roles even in modern households.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
